LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT VIRGIN ATLANTIC AIRWAYS, LTD'S MOTION TO DISMISS - THE MOTION TO DISMISS PURSUANT TO CCP 581(F)(2) IS DENIED. CCP 472B STATES IN PERTINENT PART: "WHEN A DEMURRER TO ANY PLEADING IS SUSTAINED OR OVERRULED, AND TIME TO AMEND OR ANSWER IS GIVEN, THE TIME SO GIVEN RUNS FROM THE SERVICE OF NOTICE OF THE DECISION OR ORDER, UNLESS THE NOTICE IS WAIVED IN OPEN COURT, AND THE WAIVER ENTERED IN THE MINUTES." DEFENDANT HAS NOT ESTABLISHED THAT IT EVER PROVIDED SERVICE OF NOTICE OF THE COURT'S ORDER FILED JUNE 24, 2013. PLAINTIFF WAS NOT PRESENT IN THE COURTROOM WHEN THE ORDER WAS SIGNED ON JUNE 24, 2013. FURTHER, EVEN IF NOTICE HAD BEEN PROVIDED BY HAND ON JUNE 24, 2013, FILING AN AMENDED COMPLAINT ON JULY 5, 2013 IS TIMELY. CCP 12 STATES THAT "THE TIME IN WHICH ANY ACT PROVIDED BY LAW IS TO BE DONE IS COMPUTED BY EXCLUDING THE FIRST DAY AND INCLUDING THE LAST, UNLESS THE LAST DAY IS A HOLIDAY, AND THEN IT IS ALSO EXCLUDED." BY THIS CALCULATION, JUNE 25 WAS THE FIRST DAY AND JULY 4, 2013 WAS THE LAST DAY AND BECAUSE IT WAS A HOLIDAY, JULY 5, 2013 WAS THE LAST DAY TO FILE. THE MOTION IS THUS COMPLETELY WITHOUT MERIT. A COPY OF THE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT WAS SERVED IN OPEN COURT TO MS. DUPRAY. RESPONSE TO BE FILED IN ACCORDANCE WITH THE CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185 =302/MJM

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-24-2013 Sustained
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT VIRGIN ATLANTIC AIRWAYS, LTD'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Appearances: Andrea B. Dupray of Clyde & Co. (415.365.9800) appearing for defendant and moving party; Christian K. Jensen (no card provided) plaintiff and responding party appearing in propria persona.

Argued and the Court passes the matter to review the proof of service. Upon the case being recalled, Ms. Dupray is present for defendant. Plaintiff is not present. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Sustained, without leave to amend. No opposition filed. Order signed. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Tony Vaughn CSR #6185 (302/EHG)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-09-2013 Continued To Nov-06-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Dismiss
Off Calendar Sep-13-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - CHRISTIAN K. JENSEN, TEL. NO. 236-0709, PLAINTIFF APPEARING IN PRO PER;
ATTY. ANDREA B. DUPRAY OF CLYDE & COMPANY, TEL. NO. 365-9800 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT VIRGIN ATLANTIC AIRWAYS, LTD's Motion To Dismiss - The motion to dismiss pursuant to CCP 581(f)(2) is denied. CCP 472b states in pertinent part: "When a demurrer to any pleading is sustained or overruled, and time to amend or answer is given, the time so given runs from the service of notice of the decision or order, unless the notice is waived in open court, and the waiver entered in the minutes." Defendant has not established that it ever provided service of notice of the Court's order filed June 24, 2013. Plaintiff was not present in the courtroom when the order was signed on June 24, 2013. Further, even if notice had been provided by hand on June 24, 2013, filing an amended complaint on July 5, 2013 is timely. CCP 12 states that "The time in which any act provided by law is to be done is computed by excluding the first day and including the last, unless the last day is a holiday, and then it is also excluded." By this calculation, June 25 was the first day and July 4, 2013 was the last day and because it was a holiday, July 5, 2013 was the last day to file. The motion is thus completely without merit. A copy of the 1st Amended Complaint was served in open court to Ms. Dupray. Response to be filed in accordance with the Code of Civil Procedure. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =302/MJM
